title: cerastium tomentosum subsp. lanigerum
intro: Cerastium tomentosum subsp. lanigerum, also known as woolly mouse-ear or snow-in-summer, is a low-growing, herbaceous perennial plant native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been found to aid in wound healing and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Cerastium tomentosum subsp. lanigerum include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and promote respiratory health, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for colds and coughs. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, from minor skin irritations to more serious respiratory conditions.
